name: Missouri Dental Assistant Registration
description: Complete guide to dental assistant licensing in Missouri — endorsement steps, fees, DANB certification, and radiography permits.

address.description:
Missouri Dental Board
3605 Missouri Blvd.
P.O. Box 1367
Jefferson City, MO 65102

requiredDocuments.name: Proof of Training
requiredDocuments.description: Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.

requiredDocuments.name: DANB Certification
requiredDocuments.description: Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.

requiredDocuments.name: Radiography Certification
requiredDocuments.description: Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

mainEntity.name: Does Missouri require a dental assistant license?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, Missouri requires Expanded Functions Dental Assistant through the state board.

mainEntity.name: Is DANB certification recognized in Missouri?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in Missouri.

mainEntity.name: Can I take dental x-rays in Missouri?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

mainEntity.name: How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in Missouri?

acceptedAnswer.text: State application fees are $10. Additional costs may include DANB CDA exam ($450), radiography certification, and background check fees.
